_meta.purpose: Public Chinese URLs whose Wayback Machine capture timeline is reconstructed for deletions and silent redactions (collectors/wayback_vantage.py). The Internet Archive is a retroactive, outside-the-wall observer: for any URL it crawled, its CDX timeline brackets a takedown or rewrite far tighter than we can from live polling, with a permanent citable snapshot on each side.
_meta.safety: TOPIC / INSTITUTION-LEVEL ONLY, per docs/ETHICS.md — never an individual's page assembled to profile a person. Every entry is a public encyclopedia entry, official portal, or state-media article. Reads hit the Internet Archive only, never Chinese infrastructure and never a person. This is a documented STARTER list (like config/github_refuge_watchlist.json and undertext DEFAULT_SURFACES); a deployer extends it, and each URL's reachability is itself recorded.
_meta.schema: each entry: {url, term (the DDTI handle), domain (DDTI hint), why}

watchlist.why: Youth unemployment rate — the statistic whose publication was suspended in 2023; watch the state encyclopedia entry for silent rewrites.
Unfinished/stalled housing — a contested economic-distress topic prone to encyclopedic softening.
The White Paper protests — an event whose public record is a censorship target.

watchlist.term: 国家统计局 最新发布
watchlist.why: National Bureau of Statistics latest-releases index — reconstruct which release pages appear then vanish.
